business.headline: Why file your ITR before July 31? These 3 benefits could helpWith the July 31 ITR deadline approaching, taxpayers have another reason to avoid delays. Filing your return on time can unlock several benefits that could prove useful throughout the year.

India responds to proposed US tariffs as trade pact negotiations continueWhile discussions over the tariff proposal continue, India and the United States are also working to finalise a broader trade agreement.

CCPA fines PhysicsWallah and McAfee for using dark patterns on digital platformsCCPA has also directed both companies to remove dark pattern practices that influenced consumer choices on their digital platforms.

Only 1% left to resolve in India- US trade pact, says US envoy Sergio GorSpeaking at Citi's 2026 India Conference on Wednesday, Gor said both countries were working to resolve the remaining "one per cent" sticking point and were hopeful of completing the agreement within the next several weeks.

Sensex ends 304 points lower as crude nears $100, IT stocks crackThe S&P BSE Sensex ended 303.67 points lower, or 0.41%, at 74,346.17, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 77.95 points, or 0.33%, to close at 23,405.60.

Big relief for airlines as govt caps jet fuel prices, clears Rs 10,000-crore fundThe decision comes amid recent developments in West Asia that have pushed up international ATF prices and increased pressure on airlines.

RBI MPC meeting starts today: Will rising inflation risks lead to a rate hike?The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) begins its three-day meeting today amid rising concerns over inflation, higher crude oil prices and global uncertainties. Will these factors be enough to trigger a rate hike?

Not correct: RBI rejects $12-billion gold sale report, says reserves unchangedIn a clarification issued on Tuesday, the RBI shared reports suggesting it had sold part of its gold holdings were "not correct" and highlighted that its physical stock of gold remains unchanged at 880.52 tonnes.

Want to use EPFO's UPI feature? Fix these 5 things firstImagine accessing your PF savings as easily as making a UPI payment. That's the experience EPFO is aiming to offer soon. However, the new feature will work smoothly only if your account details are accurate. Experts recommend checking five key areas now to avoid problems later.

Gold, silver prices are down again. Should investors buy, hold or wait?If you've been tracking gold and silver prices recently, you've probably noticed that the rally has lost some steam. Both metals have slipped despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, leaving investors with an important decision to make: buy more, stay invested, or wait for the dust to settle.

TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech crash up to 8%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell sharply on Wednesday as investors booked profits after a two-day rally. Weak ADR cues, cooling global software momentum and uncertainty over AI gains weighed on sentiment.

US proposes up to 12.5% tariff on India, China over forced labour concernsThe proposed action, announced by the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR), comes under Section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974, the same law that was used to impose tariffs on Chinese imports during President Donald Trump's first term.

Sensex crashes 700 points. What's behind today's market meltdown?Markets opened sharply lower as renewed Iran-US tensions, higher crude prices and heavy IT profit-booking hit trade on Wednesday. The weaker rupee, persistent FPI outflow concerns and the RBI policy outlook kept volatility elevated.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade over crude oil price rise; TCS falls 6%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 763.45 points, or 1.02%, to 73,886.39 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 213.75 points, or 0.91%, to 23,269.80.

IndusInd Bank faces fresh whistleblower allegations; complaint sent to PMO, RBIA whistleblower complaint has sought a probe into alleged insider trading and governance lapses at IndusInd Bank. The allegations add fresh scrutiny as the lender remains under watch over its Rs 2,000-crore derivatives accounting discrepancy.

Not just the AI king. Nvidia may be the world's safest companyNvidia's credit default swap has traded close to US sovereign levels as its market value and earnings surge. The reading reflects unusually strong confidence in the AI chipmaker's ability to withstand shocks and meet obligations.

US wants to end Russian oil sanctions waiver: How it will hit IndiaThe US has repeatedly criticised purchases of Russian oil since the Ukraine conflict began, claiming that revenues from crude exports help fund Moscow's military campaign.

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Oyo parent Prism gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650 crore IPO, targets up to $8 billion valuationFollowing Sebi's approval, the company is now expected to file an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-1), which will be made available for public review and comments.

Waiting for Form 16? Here's when you'll receive it and why it matters for your ITRThe countdown to ITR filing has begun, and Form 16 remains one of the most important documents for salaried taxpayers. Knowing when your employer must issue it and what information it contains can help you prepare for a smoother tax filing experience.

entertainment.headline: Supergirl final trailer: Milly Alcock defies Superman; Jason Momoa joins forces The final trailer for Supergirl shows Kara Zor-El teaming with Ruthye while clashing with Superman's wider legacy. It also brings Jason Momoa's Lobo into the DC Universe and expands the story of Krypton and the House of El.

Is Allu Arjun-Lokesh Kanagaraj's AA23 shelved? Writer Rathna Kumar clarifies Writer Rathna Kumar shared a social media post about Allu Arjun-Lokesh Kanagaraj's AA23 amid speculations about the film getting shelved.

Peddi fever begins: Ram Charan fans burst crackers at preview shows. Video Ram Charan's Peddi drew enthusiastic fans to special preview shows at Bengaluru's Brunda theatre. The turnout gave the screenings a festive mood ahead of the film's release.

How Obsession's most disturbing scene was shot using a doll and jelly-glass A behind-the-scenes footage shows how Obsession staged its most disturbing attack with practical effects. The making of the scene highlights how a doll, fake blood and jelly-like glass created its impact.

When Aamir Khan said that marriage may not suit him at 60 Aamir Khan is set to formalise his relationship with Gauri Spratt in an intimate registered marriage. The development has revived his earlier remark that getting married at 60 would not suit him.

Mukesh Ambani impressed by Dhurandhar's Rs 3,000 crore: 40% of India's box office Mukesh Ambani praised the Dhurandhar franchise for its unprecedented box office success, contributing 40% of India's total collections and setting new industry benchmarks.

Peddi advance booking: Ram Charan's sports drama earns Rs 35 crore globally Ram Charan's Peddi has grossed Rs 35 crore worldwide in advance sales before its June 4 release. The early trend highlights strong Telugu market support as the film faces a key test beyond its core base.

Anushka Sharma says homeopathy helped her, sparks age-old medicine debate Anushka Sharma shared a video of Dr Rajan Sankaran and said homeopathy shaped her health journey. The post split social media, with critics questioning celebrity influence and supporters calling it personal choice.

Long-lost friends to life partners: Timeline of Aamir Khan-Gauri Spratt relationship Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are set to formalise their relationship through a registered marriage at his home on July 5. The private ceremony follows a year of widely-discussed public appearances.

RGV owes technicians Rs 1 crore: Ashoke Pandit slams director's Don 3 row remarks Ashoke Pandit launched a scathing attack on Ram Gopal Varma, demanding an unconditional apology for his recent controversial social media posts on the Don 3 dispute.

The Gupt confession: Why Bobby Deol refused an intimate scene with Manisha Koirala Bobby Deol recalled refusing to shoot an intimate scene with Manisha Koirala during a song sequence in Gupt. He also revisited a failed prank from the sets of the 1997 film.

Ram Charan fan club trolled for idol that doesn't resemble him, apologises Ram Charan fans in Telangana's Kukatpally unveiled a Peddi-inspired idol at Arjun Theatre that drew mockery online for not resembling the actor. The backlash prompted an apology from the fan club over the misfire.

Aamir Khan, 61, to marry Gauri Spratt on July 5; SRK and Salman may attend Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt will formalise their relationship in a private registered marriage at his home on July 5. The ceremony reflects the couple's decision to begin this new chapter quietly with their families and close friends.

Dinesh Vijan jokes Shahid Kapoor's love life was a hexagon. Actor responds At the Cocktail 2 trailer launch, Dinesh Vijan joked that Shahid Kapoor's love life was a hexagon, not a triangle. This invited another candid remark from the actor, who referenced that it was rather a circle.

Don 3 row: Producers body cites Akshaye Khanna's Drishyam 3 exit, discusses conduct Amid Ranveer Singh's Don 3 row, the Producers Guild of India has cited complaints about Akshaye Khanna's departure from Drishyam 3. The actor's exit came amid Dhurandhar success.

The Voice of Hind Rajab receives censor certificate after months in limbo The Voice of Hind Rajab, a film depicting the tragic killing of a Palestinian child during the Israel-Gaza conflict, has finally received its censor certificate in India after months of delay.

Was Ranveer Singh right to serve legal notice on FWICE? What industry, lawyers say The Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) has withdrawn its non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh after the actor issued a legal notice against the film body. As Don 3 impasse heats up, lawyers and a veteran industry voice weigh in.

Ranveer Singh scores first win in Don 3 row as film body withdraws 'ban' The Federation of Western Cine Employees lifted the non-cooperation directive issued against actor Ranveer Singh following his abrupt exit from Don 3. The film body further said they would fight Singh's legal notice in court.

Pankaj Bhadouria shares recovery update, urges women to get breast cancer screening Pankaj Bhadouria shared a video update after her breast cancer surgery and thanked supporters for their prayers. She said early detection helped her recovery and urged women to get regular screenings and check-ups.

general news.headline: Iran says Beirut strike would reignite war as Trump eyes weekend breakthroughIran said any Israeli attack on Beirut would bring grave consequences and risk renewed war. The warning came as Tehran and Washington kept reviewing draft texts and Donald Trump said a deal could be close.

Delhi fire horror: Hotel owner arrested as probe into 21 deaths widensThe arrest of hotel owner Lavkesh Bajaj comes as Delhi Police probe the devastating Malviya Nagar hotel fire that killed 21 people and injured several others. Investigators have uncovered alleged safety and licensing violations, including operating far beyond approved capacity and running the establishment without a fire NOC.

Iran targets US military vessel hosting command centre in Sea of OmanAccording to Iran's Tasnim news agency, the vessel was hosting a "command and control centre" and was approaching Iranian territorial waters when it was targeted.

Netanyahu asked about Trump calling him 'f***ing crazy' over Lebanon. What he saidNetanyahu says disagreements with Trump are limited to tactics rather than fundamental differences, after the US President confirmed an Axios report that he used the expletive during a phone call with the Israeli PM.

How a first-time TMC MLA got 60 MLAs to rebel against Mamata in weeksHandcrafted ByOur Editors A rebel Trinamool faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee submitted signatures of 60 MLAs to back him as Leader of the Opposition. The move has raised questions about how a first-time TMC MLA managed to rally 60 of the 80 legislators against Mamata, and that too within weeks of the poll rout.

Suryakumar to be sacked, Shreyas Iyer in 3-way race for India's T20I captaincyShreyas Iyer is the front-runner to become India's new T20I captain for the Ireland and England series. The move would signal a long-term leadership reset, with Tilak Varma and Ishan Kishan also in contention.

Pak men who gang-raped French tourist in front of her children to be executedA French woman was inside the vehicle with the doors locked after it ran out of fuel. The attackers smashed a window, dragged her out, and raped her at gunpoint in front of her children.

Delhi govt forms panel after Malviya Nagar fire, citywide inspections from todayThe Delhi government has formed a joint committee to inspect B&Bs and guest houses after the Malviya Nagar hotel fire. The exercise could lead to sealing action as officials review safety, licencing and occupancy compliance.

Ek Bihari sab pe bhaari: RCB finally reveal Virat Kohli's chat with SooryavanshiAfter Royal Challengers Bengaluru's IPL final win, Virat Kohli sought out Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for a brief but telling exchange. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was in Ahmedabad on the day of the final after having swept the awards for his extraordinary season.

Fourth S-400 delivery reaches India, deployment likely on western frontThe S-400 system gained significant attention during Operation Sindoor in May 2025, when it played a crucial role in India's air defence operations by intercepting hostile aerial threats. Its performance reinforced confidence in the platform's capabilities and contributed to the government's decision to approve the acquisition of five additional S-400 squadrons, which would eventually double India's planned inventory to ten units.

BJP should back Annamalai's new party. A subsidiary model for double engineTaking a leaf out of the Volkswagen-Skoda book, the BJP should back K Annamalai's new party to have an entity from the same production line but with a different badge in Tamil Nadu. The BJP's Hindi-Hindutva plank has slid against the Tamil sub-nationalism wall, and Annamalai has all that is needed to capture the Dravidian fortress.

Expelled TMC MLA Ritabrata becomes LoP, says Mamata remains our chiefThe development came after Ritabrata Banerjee staked claim to the post of Leader of the Opposition, in defiance of the TMC leadership, despite having been expelled from the party just two days ago.

Homeopathy is witchcraft, says Liver Doc to Anushka Sharma's endorsement postAnushka Sharma's endorsement of homeopathy has reignited debate, drawing praise from practitioners and criticism from evidence-based medicine advocates, such as The Liver Doc.

Rs 1 crore cash, bedrooms, gun, condoms in Kolkata college. Tip of the iceberg?Kolkata's Surendranath College, a prestigious institution deeply intertwined with the freedom movement, has found itself engulfed in a massive controversy after cash, liquor bottles and a revolver were recovered. It has brought into focus the issue of alleged misuse of power in Bengal educational institutions.

Siddu's long shadow will make Shivakumar rule uneasy. Here's whyThe Congress high command has successfully averted an immediate crisis by crowning DK Shivakumar the Chief Minister of Karnataka, requesting Siddaramiah to step aside. But, Siddu has support from a majority of the MLAs and powerful community leaders. This is why keeping the CM's job could be a real challenge for DKS.

SpiceJet's Pune-Dubai flight delayed overnight, passengers strandedPassengers on SpiceJet flight SG 51 from Pune to Dubai said they were left waiting overnight after repeated delays. The complaints centred on conflicting explanations, poor communication and inadequate assistance at the airport.

How Obsession's most disturbing scene was shot using a doll and jelly-glassA behind-the-scenes footage shows how Obsession staged its most disturbing attack with practical effects. The making of the scene highlights how a doll, fake blood and jelly-like glass created its impact.

700 recruits, 12 projects, one vanished company: Inside Pune's IT job fraudPune Police have arrested Thynk Technology India CEO Harshal Thakare in an alleged IT recruitment scam after hundreds of engineering graduates, freshers and interns claimed they were duped with promises of jobs, stipends and permanent employment before the company abruptly shut down operations.

Want to quit tennis right now: Sabalenka drops a bomb after stunning exitRoland Garros: Aryna Sabalenka said she wanted to quit tennis after blowing a set-and-break lead to Diana Shnaider in the French Open quarter-final. Her remarks laid bare the mental strain of repeated setbacks on clay and grass.

Video: Terrified guests jump from upper floors as fire tears through Delhi hotelA viral video from the deadly hotel fire in South Delhi's Malviya Nagar, which killed 21 people, shows trapped occupants jumping from upper floors in a desperate attempt to escape the flames. Locals gather below, helping cushion the guests' fall and pulling them to safety amid the chaos.

Noida's midnight crowd is lining up for roadside gourmet pizzaWhen it comes to gourmet wood-fired pizza in Delhi-NCR, names like Leo's, Da Susy, and Evoo are often the first that come to mind. But here's a surprise: street-side carts in Noida are now serving their own gourmet takes on pizza, bringing artisanal flavours and wood-fired-style creations to the city's bustling food scene.

I was perturbed: Trump admits calling Netanyahu 'f***ing crazy' over Beirut attacksDonald Trump said he confronted Benjamin Netanyahu over Israel's military actions in Lebanon and admitted calling him 'crazy' during a phone call. The exchange highlighted how the Lebanon fighting was complicating US-Iran talks as fresh attacks widened the regional conflict.

Friend helps man murder family for property, then kills him using same rod over lootA blood-soaked home, a missing son, a handwritten clue pointing at the wrong people and a murder probe that kept changing direction. What police uncovered next turned a family massacre into an extraordinary tale of deception.

What is Delhi's O-Zone? Why 15 lakh homes facing demolition riskDelhi's O-Zone, which covers the Yamuna floodplain from Wazirabad to Okhla, includes 94 colonies and nearly 15 lakh homes that authorities classify as unauthorised under the Master Plan 2021.

sports.headline: Pakistan Women face reality check before World Cup with Ireland series shocker Pakistan Women endured a disappointing Ireland tri-series campaign, failing to reach the final after a washout against West Indies on Wednesday. They will now shift focus to preparations for the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 with two warm-up matches in Derby.

Arnav Paparkar's dream run ends with third round defeat in French Open boys singles French Open 2026: Arnav Paparkar's promising Roland Garros 2026 Boys Singles run ended in the third round, as he went down 7-5, 6-2 to fourth seed Jack Kennedy in the Round of 16.

Flavio Cobolli fights past Felix Auger-Aliassime to reach maiden Grand Slam semis French Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli delivered the defining win of his career, rallying past Felix Auger-Aliassime 4-6, 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 at Roland Garros to reach his maiden Grand Slam semifinal.

FIFA World Cup: Switzerland's Embolo awaits US clearance after visa submission Switzerland forward Breel Embolo applied for an emergency U.S. visa at the American embassy in Bern on Wednesday, a day after he was denied boarding the national team's World Cup-bound flight because of a criminal conviction that recently became legally binding.

Praggnanandhaa relishes having a shot at title after ending Carlsen hopes Fresh from his second win over Magnus Carlsen, R Praggnanandhaa said he was happy to remain in the title race as Norway Chess enters its final two rounds after the rest day, with the Indian Grandmaster relishing his chance to fight for the crown.

Want to quit tennis right now: Sabalenka drops a bomb after stunning exit Roland Garros: Aryna Sabalenka said she wanted to quit tennis after blowing a set-and-break lead to Diana Shnaider in the French Open quarter-final. Her remarks laid bare the mental strain of repeated setbacks on clay and grass.

Sooryavanshi is a special talent, should not change anything: Virat Kohli's coach Superstar Virat Kohli's childhood coach, Rajkumar Sharma, has described teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as the "rarest of the rare" talent in T20 cricket, urging the youngster to stay true to his fearless approach and avoid making any changes to his game.

MPL helping state talent turn IPL dreams into reality: MPCA President Madhya Pradesh Cricket Association (MPCA) President Mahanaaryaman Scindia said the addition of new franchises in MPL has opened up more doors for young cricketers across the state, giving them a larger platform to showcase their skills and accelerate their cricketing journey.

Mandhana inspires me, Richa Ghosh always supports me: Bengal's Ankita Chakraborty Ankita Chakraborty said Smriti Mandhana inspires her while Richa Ghosh remains a trusted source of support ahead of the Bengal T20 League. The Adamas Howrah Warriors captain also highlighted how family values, Bengal's cricket structure and team balance shape her plans for the season.

World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka stunned as Diana Shnaider pulls off huge upset French Open Live Updates  Diana Shnaider scripted one of the biggest upsets of Roland Garros 2026, rallying from 3-6, 3-5 down to stun World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 3-6, 7-5, 6-0 and reach her maiden Grand Slam semi-final.

Suryakumar to be sacked, Shreyas Iyer in 3-way race for India's T20I captaincyShreyas Iyer is the front-runner to become India's new T20I captain for the Ireland and England series. The move would signal a long-term leadership reset, with Tilak Varma and Ishan Kishan also in contention.

Indian national anthem in Greece: Atiqa Mir races to most dominant win of her careerAtiqa Mir swept qualifying, heats and finals in Greece to seal her most dominant international karting win. The Indian anthem played on the podium as the 11-year-old underlined her world-stage promise.

Archer loves playing for England: Stokes defends pacer's IPL-enforced absenceEngland captain Ben Stokes has thrown his support behind Jofra Archer, saying the fast bowler remains fully committed to England despite missing the first Test against New Zealand due to his IPL commitments.

Mohammed Siraj bowls full tilt on hot day in Chandigarh, eases concerns over injuryIndia vs Afghanistan: Mohammed Siraj bowled at full tilt in oppressive New Chandigarh heat, easing fitness worries before India's Afghanistan Test. His spell strengthens India's plans with Jasprit Bumrah absent and selection calls still taking shape.

Group F preview: Samurai speed, total football, styles clash awaitsFIFA World Cup 2026, Group F Preview: The Oranje's Total Football meets Japan's high-speed press, Sweden's lethal attack and Tunisia looking to cause havoc. Group F is a tactical masterclass where the top dogs must deliver.

No Iga Swiatek, but Maja Chwalinska keeps Polish flag flying with dream runFrench Open 2026: With Iga Swiatek out of the tournament, qualifier Maja Chwalinska has emerged as Poland's unlikely new hope, reaching the Roland Garros semi-finals with a stunning win over Anna Kalinskaya.

Ayush Shetty stages sensational comeback win, Prannoy crashes outAyush Shetty recovered from a poor start to beat Weng Hongyang and reach the last 16. The win gave India some relief after Satwik-Chirag's injury exit, even as HS Prannoy fell short.

Ek Bihari sab pe bhaari: RCB finally reveal Virat Kohli's chat with SooryavanshiAfter Royal Challengers Bengaluru's IPL final win, Virat Kohli sought out Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for a brief but telling exchange. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was in Ahmedabad on the day of the final after having swept the awards for his extraordinary season.

Archer has no interest in Tests: Simon Doull slams pacer's England absence for IPLFormer New Zealand pacer Simon Doull has criticised Jofra Archer's absence from England's squad for the first Test against New Zealand, questioning the pacer's commitment to the longest format after he remained with Rajasthan Royals for the IPL playoffs.

Chasing immortality: The legendary veterans eyeing a final FIFA World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: The countdown is on for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where a golden generation of veterans faces its final stand. From Luka Modrić to Mohamed Salah, these ageing masters must defy Father Time and brutal summer heat for one last shot at glory.

Satwik's injury halts Sat-Chi at Indonesia Open days after ending title droughtSatwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty retired midway through their Indonesia Open opener with Satwik clutching his right shoulder. The setback came two days after their Singapore Open title, shifting focus to recovery before major events.

French Open Highlights: Cobolli eliminates Auger-Aliassime, Sabalenka outFrench Open 2026 Day 11 Highlights: Felix Auger-Aliassime has been knocked out after Flavio Cobolli defeated him in the quarters of the men's singles. Earlier, Diana Shnaider knocked World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka in the quarterfinals. Maja Chwalinska also knocked out Anna Kalinskaya. The qualifier beat the seeded Russian in straight sets.

F1 2026: Charles Leclerc extends stay with Ferrari, signs multi-year extensionFerrari has tied Charles Leclerc down to a new multi-year deal ahead of his home race in Monaco. Leclerc has been the best driver for Ferrari this season and is challenging Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli and George Russell.

Did Vaibhav Sooryavanshi insult Harsha Bhogle? Commentator calls out viral clipHarsha Bhogle has dismissed a viral video claiming Vaibhav Sooryavanshi insulted Virat Kohli. The clip depicts Sooryavanshi as brash and arrogant but Bhogle called out the creators for their attempt to tarnish the young prodigy's reputation.

Hype the entire Women's T20 World Cup, not just IND vs PAK: Smriti MandhanaIndia vice-captain Smriti Mandhana urged fans and media to hype the entire Women's T20 World Cup rather than focusing solely on the high-profile opening group match against rivals Pakistan.

Haryana's wrestling monks: How one state claimed all 18 Asian Games spotsHaryana's wrestlers have claimed every available place in India's Asian Games squad after dominating the trials. The sweep from the athletes shows how the state, deep-rooted in their traditional methods, can dominate the rest.

India to play biggest-ever bilateral tour of New Zealand in landmark yearIndia will tour New Zealand for a 12-match all-format series from October 22 to December 1. The tour will also mark 100 years of sporting ties between both nations.

Shreyas Iyer's misery continues as Shivam Dube steals the show in Mumbai T20 LeagueShreyas Iyer's batting struggles continued in the Mumbai T20 League opener, where he was dismissed for just five runs by Shivam Dube, triggering a major batting collapse for his team.

French Open Day 11 Schedule: Sabalenka in action, Indian talent on double dutyWorld No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka and an all-Italian night session headline the French Open quarterfinals, while Indian stars Sriram Balaji and Arnav Paparkar chase historic wins in Paris.

Meet Rasikh Salam: The boy Irfan Pathan spotted. The man RCB trustedIPL 2026: Irfan Pathan saw something in a teenager walking away from a trial in Srinagar. Eight years, four franchises and a two-year ban later, Rasikh Salam took 19 wickets in a title-winning campaign for Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Here is his story.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
